records.ety: [Of imitative origin; cf. OE. pipen, F. piper, ppier, L. pipire, pipare, pipiare, D. & G. piepen. Senses 2 and 3 perhaps come from a transfer of sense from the sound which chickens make upon the first breaking of the shell to the act accompanying it; or perhaps from the influence of peek, or peak. Cf. Pipe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Peeped (pēpt); p. pr. & vb. n. Peeping.]

defs: To cry, as a chicken hatching or newly hatched; to chirp; to cheep.
quotes: There was none that moved the wing, or opened the mouth, or peeped.

defs: To begin to appear; to look forth from concealment; to make the first appearance; as, the sun peeped over the eastern hills.
quotes: When flowers first peeped, and trees did blossoms bear.

defs: To look cautiously or slyly; to peer, as through a crevice; to pry.
quotes: Peep through the blanket of the dark.

defs: The cry of a young chicken; a chirp.

quotes: Oft have we seen him at the peep of dawn.

defs: A sly look; a look as through a crevice, or from a place of concealment.
quotes: To take t' other peep at the stars.

defs: Any small sandpiper, as the least sandpiper (Trigna minutilla).
